prompts.text: {player1}, boarding pass accepted! Take 2 sips — your flight is now departing.
{player1}, you're the tour guide tonight. Give out 3 sips to the group.
{player1}, welcome aboard. Name a country you'd visit tomorrow. Take 1 sip.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever needs a vacation the most.
{player1}, fasten your seatbelt. Take 2 sips of turbulence.
{player1}, you just landed. Give out 3 sips to whoever looks the most jet-lagged.
First to shout it out: What is the capital of Australia? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Canberra — NOT Sydney or Melbourne

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the capital of Brazil? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Brasília — NOT Rio de Janeiro or São Paulo

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the capital of Turkey? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Ankara — NOT Istanbul

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the capital of Canada? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Ottawa — NOT Toronto or Vancouver

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the capital of Switzerland? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Bern — NOT Zurich or Geneva

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the capital of Myanmar? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Naypyidaw — a purpose-built capital that replaced Yangon in 2006. Almost nobody gets this.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the capital of South Africa? Trick question — there are 3. Name any? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Pretoria (executive), Cape Town (legislative), Bloemfontein (judicial) — South Africa has 3 capitals

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the most time zones? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: France — 12 time zones when including overseas territories (New Caledonia, French Polynesia, etc.)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has more pyramids than Egypt? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Sudan — it has over 200 pyramids, more than double Egypt's ~130

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only country whose flag is not rectangular? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Nepal — it has two stacked triangles

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country is in all 4 hemispheres at once? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Kiribati — it spans the equator AND the International Date Line

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Which African country was never colonized by a European power? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Ethiopia (and Liberia) — Ethiopia successfully resisted Italian colonization at the Battle of Adwa in 1896

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the smallest country in the world? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Vatican City — only 0.44 km² (about 121 acres), smaller than most golf courses

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest country by area that most people forget about? It's #1? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Russia — 17.1 million km². It spans 11 time zones and covers more area than Pluto's surface.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the driest place on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Atacama Desert in Chile — some weather stations there have NEVER recorded rain

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country is both in Europe AND Asia? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Turkey (also Russia, Kazakhstan, Georgia, and Azerbaijan straddle both continents)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the longest river in the world? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Nile — approximately 6,650 km (though the Amazon is sometimes disputed as longer)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the deepest lake in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Lake Baikal in Russia — 1,642 meters deep. It holds 20% of the world's unfrozen fresh water.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ocean is the deepest? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Pacific Ocean — the Mariana Trench reaches nearly 11,000 meters (36,000 feet) deep

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most visited country in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: France — over 89 million international tourists per year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Africa is bigger than which combination of countries? USA, China, India, and most of Europe combined? True or false? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: True — Africa is 30.3 million km². The Mercator projection makes it look way smaller than it is.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the tallest mountain in the world measured from base to peak (not sea level)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mauna Kea in Hawaii — 10,210 meters from its oceanic base (though Everest is highest above sea level at 8,849m)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of the Earth's surface is covered by water? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: About 71%

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many countries are there in the world (UN recognized)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 193 UN member states (plus 2 observer states: Vatican City and Palestine)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most landlocked country in the world (most countries between it and the ocean)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Liechtenstein and Uzbekistan — both are 'doubly landlocked' (surrounded only by other landlocked countries)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the longest coastline in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Canada — over 202,000 km of coastline, more than any other country by far

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Japan, what does it mean if you stick your chopsticks upright in rice? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It resembles incense at a funeral — it's considered extremely rude and associated with death

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Thailand, what body part is it deeply offensive to point at someone with? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Your feet — the feet are considered the lowest and dirtiest part of the body

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In India, which hand should you NEVER eat with? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The left hand — it's considered unclean in many South Asian cultures

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country is it illegal to chew gum in? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Singapore — importing and selling gum has been banned since 1992 (with some medical exceptions)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Finland, what unusual tradition involves beating yourself with birch branches? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The sauna — Finns use birch branch bundles (vihta) to lightly beat themselves, improving circulation

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In which country is it customary to slurp your noodles loudly to show appreciation? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Japan — slurping ramen, soba, or udon is considered polite and shows the food is good

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Colombia, what time does '8 PM' usually mean when someone invites you to a party? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 9:30-10 PM — showing up on time in Latin American social settings is considered odd/early

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Bulgaria, what does shaking your head side to side mean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Yes — and nodding means no. The gestures are reversed from most of the world.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country celebrates a 'Tomato Festival' where people throw tomatoes at each other? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Spain — La Tomatina in Buñol, held annually on the last Wednesday of August

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what country do people celebrate New Year's Eve by wearing different colored underwear for luck? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Brazil (and several Latin American countries) — yellow for money, red for love, white for peace

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Maori greeting in New Zealand involves pressing what together? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Noses and foreheads — it's called a hongi, and it symbolizes the sharing of breath of life

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country drinks the most coffee per capita? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Finland — Finns consume about 12 kg of coffee per person per year, nearly double the US

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country drinks the most beer per capita? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Czech Republic (Czechia) — about 188 liters per person per year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Sushi actually refers to what? The fish or the rice? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The rice — 'sushi' literally means 'sour rice.' The vinegared rice is the sushi; the fish is sashimi.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: French fries are NOT from France. Where are they actually from? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Belgium — they originated in the Meuse valley region. American soldiers in WWI called them 'French' because they were in French-speaking Belgium.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Mexican food was actually invented in the United States? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The hard-shell taco — Glen Bell (Taco Bell founder) popularized the crispy U-shaped shell. Traditional Mexican tacos use soft tortillas.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What fruit is illegal to bring on public transport in Singapore and many Southeast Asian hotels? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Durian — it's banned from many public spaces because of its extremely strong smell

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Ethiopia, what do they traditionally serve coffee in during a coffee ceremony? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A jebena (clay coffee pot) — the Ethiopian coffee ceremony can last 2-3 hours and is a major social ritual. Ethiopia is the birthplace of coffee.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the world's most expensive spice by weight? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Saffron — it can cost $5,000-$10,000 per pound because each crocus flower produces only 3 tiny stigma threads

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In which country would you find 'cuy' (roasted guinea pig) as a traditional delicacy? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Peru (and Ecuador, Colombia, Bolivia) — guinea pigs have been a food source in the Andes for thousands of years

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country invented ice cream? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: China — a frozen milk and rice mixture existed as early as 200 BC. Marco Polo is rumored to have brought the concept to Italy.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most spoken language in the world by total speakers? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: English — about 1.5 billion speakers (when counting non-native speakers). Mandarin has the most NATIVE speakers.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What language has the most words? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: English — the Oxford English Dictionary contains over 170,000 words in current use

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many languages are spoken in Papua New Guinea, the most linguistically diverse country? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 840 languages — for a population of only about 9 million people

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the official language of Brazil? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Portuguese — NOT Spanish. Brazil is the only Portuguese-speaking country in the Americas.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What writing system does Japanese use? There are 3 — name them? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hiragana, Katakana, and Kanji — Japanese uses all three systems simultaneously

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long did it take to build the Great Wall of China? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 2,000 years — construction began in the 7th century BC and continued through the Ming Dynasty (1644 AD)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Eiffel Tower was originally supposed to be torn down after how many years? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 20 years — it was built for the 1889 World's Fair and slated for demolition in 1909. It was saved because it was useful as a radio antenna.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Machu Picchu was 'lost' for centuries. What year was it 'rediscovered' by Hiram Bingham? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 1911 — though local farmers always knew it was there. Bingham was guided there by an 11-year-old boy.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color was the Statue of Liberty when it was first unveiled? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Copper/brown — the green color (patina) developed over decades from oxidation of the copper skin

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Leaning Tower of Pisa started leaning during construction. On which floor? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: After the third floor — the soft ground on one side caused it to tilt. Construction took 199 years partly because of wars, giving the soil time to settle.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many people visit the Louvre in Paris each year, making it the world's most visited museum? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 7-10 million visitors per year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the tallest building in the world as of 2024? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Burj Khalifa in Dubai — 828 meters (2,717 feet) tall with 163 floors

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Stonehenge is made of massive stones. Where did the smaller 'bluestones' come from? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Preseli Hills in Wales — about 150 miles away. How Neolithic people transported 4-ton stones that far remains a mystery.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ancient wonder of the world is the only one still standing? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Great Pyramid of Giza — the oldest of the Seven Wonders and the only survivor

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country used to be called Persia? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Iran — it officially changed its name in 1935

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What European country didn't give women the right to vote until 1971? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Switzerland — and some cantons held out until 1990

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Iceland has no army, navy, or air force. True or false? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: True — Iceland has no standing military. NATO and the US provided defense during the Cold War.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has a flag with an AK-47 on it? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mozambique — the flag features a Kalashnikov rifle, a hoe, and a book

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the oldest continuously inhabited city in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Damascus, Syria — evidence of habitation dates back over 11,000 years

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How old is the United States compared to the oldest universities? Oxford University predates the Aztec Empire. True or false? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: True — Oxford was teaching by 1096 AD. The Aztec Empire was founded around 1428 AD.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Italy — with 58 sites, followed by China (57) and Germany (52)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What European country is technically at war with itself and has been since 1651? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Netherlands — the Isles of Scilly vs Netherlands 'war' was never formally ended until 1986. 335 years of 'war' with zero casualties.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the busiest airport in the world by passenger traffic? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport — over 93 million passengers per year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the longest commercial flight in the world (nonstop)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Singapore to New York (JFK) on Singapore Airlines — approximately 18.5 hours, covering over 15,000 km

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway in the world. How long is the full journey? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 9,289 km from Moscow to Vladivostok — the journey takes about 6 days nonstop

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country drives on the left side of the road besides the UK? Name one? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Japan, Australia, India, Thailand, South Africa, and about 70 other countries — roughly 35% of the world drives on the left

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most dangerous road in the world, nicknamed 'Death Road'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: North Yungas Road in Bolivia — a narrow, cliff-hugging road with 600m drops and no guardrails. About 200-300 deaths per year before a new road was built.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country uses the 'Yen' as currency? Name both countries that use it? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Japan (¥) — and China uses the 'Yuan' (also ¥). They share the same symbol but are different currencies.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the strongest currency in the world (highest value per unit)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Kuwaiti Dinar — 1 KWD equals roughly $3.25 USD

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the most billionaires? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The United States — followed by China and India

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the poorest country in the world by GDP per capita? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Burundi or South Sudan (depending on the year) — under $300 GDP per capita

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest desert in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Antarctica — it's a polar desert. The Sahara is the largest HOT desert.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What waterfall has the highest single drop in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Angel Falls in Venezuela — 979 meters (3,212 feet). The water is so high it often turns to mist before reaching the bottom.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most remote inhabited island in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tristan da Cunha — a British territory in the South Atlantic, 2,400 km from the nearest land (Saint Helena). Population: about 250.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the most volcanoes? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Indonesia — with about 130 active volcanoes, it sits on the Pacific Ring of Fire

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the flattest country in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Maldives — its highest natural point is only about 2.4 meters (8 feet) above sea level

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the saltiest body of water on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Don Juan Pond in Antarctica — over 40% salinity. The Dead Sea is about 34%.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country contains the northernmost point of mainland Africa? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tunisia — Ras ben Sakka (or Cap Angela) is the northernmost point

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What river flows through the most capital cities? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Danube — it flows through 4 capitals: Vienna (Austria), Bratislava (Slovakia), Budapest (Hungary), and Belgrade (Serbia)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most populated city in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tokyo — the Greater Tokyo Area has over 37 million people

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the longest life expectancy? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Japan — average life expectancy of about 84 years, with Okinawa being famous for centenarians

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the youngest average population in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Niger — median age of about 15 years old

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of the world's population lives in the Northern Hemisphere? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 90% — the vast majority of Earth's land and population is in the Northern Hemisphere

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Indonesia has how many islands? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 17,000 islands — about 6,000 of which are inhabited

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What two countries share the longest international border? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Canada and the United States — 8,891 km (5,525 miles) including the Great Lakes

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only country that borders both France and Brazil? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Suriname — NO! Trick question. The answer is actually NEITHER — but French Guiana (a French territory) borders Brazil. So technically, France borders Brazil via French Guiana.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Russia has the most international borders with other countries. How many? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 16 countries — including Norway, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Belarus, Ukraine, Georgia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, China, Mongolia, North Korea, plus Japan and the US by water

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What strait separates Europe from Asia at its narrowest point? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Bosphorus — only 700 meters wide at its narrowest, running through Istanbul

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what country do they run with bulls through the streets? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Spain — the Running of the Bulls (San Fermín) in Pamplona, held annually in July

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country celebrates Diwali, the festival of lights? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: India (and Nepal, Sri Lanka, and many countries with Hindu populations) — it celebrates the triumph of light over darkness

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Carnival in Rio de Janeiro features a parade competition between what groups? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Samba schools — neighborhood-based organizations that compete with elaborate floats, costumes, and choreography

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What holiday in Mexico celebrates the dead with altars, marigolds, and sugar skulls? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead) — November 1-2. It's a celebration, not mourning.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country holds Oktoberfest, and what city is it in? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Germany — Munich (München). Despite its name, it mostly takes place in September.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Holi, the festival of colors, originates from what religion? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hinduism — it celebrates the arrival of spring and the triumph of good over evil

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country fits inside its neighbor 462 times? The small one is a European microstate? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: San Marino inside Italy — San Marino is 61 km² while Italy is 301,340 km²

prompts.text: First to shout it out: North Korea and Finland are separated by how many countries? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: One — Russia. Finland borders Russia's west, North Korea borders Russia's east.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: There's a town in Norway called 'Hell.' What happens there in winter? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hell freezes over — literally. It's a real place and it gets below freezing in winter. They sell postcards that say 'Hell — God Expedition.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has more sheep than people? The ratio is about 5:1? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: New Zealand — approximately 26 million sheep for 5 million people

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what country can you find a town literally named 'Batman'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Turkey — Batman is a city in southeastern Turkey with about 600,000 people. The mayor once tried to sue Warner Bros over the Batman franchise.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What European country consumes the most chocolate per capita? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Switzerland — about 8.8 kg per person per year

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Canada has more lakes than every other country in the world combined. True or false? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: True — Canada has an estimated 2 million lakes, covering about 9% of its total area

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only continent with no active volcanoes? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Australia — though it does have extinct volcanic regions

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Amazon Rainforest produces what percentage of the world's oxygen? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 6% (not 20% as commonly claimed) — most of the oxygen it produces is consumed by the forest itself. Oceans produce most of Earth's oxygen via phytoplankton.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What country has the most official languages? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Zimbabwe — 16 official languages including English, Shona, Ndebele, and 13 others. South Africa has 12.

prompts.text: {player1}, what's the most touristy thing you've ever done? Take 2 sips of shame.
{player1}, name a country you couldn't point to on a map. Take 2 sips of honesty.
{player1}, what's the worst flight experience you've ever had? Tell it or take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever gotten lost in a foreign country? Yes = take 2 sips. No = you haven't traveled enough, take 3.
{player1}, what's the weirdest food you've tried abroad? No answer = take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever missed a flight? Yes = take 3 sips. No = take 1 sip of luck.
{player1}, say 'cheers' in another language or take 3 sips.
{player1}, what country is #1 on your bucket list? If someone else has been there, they give you 2 sips.
{player1}, have you ever pretended to understand a language you don't speak? Yes = 2 sips.
{player1}, do an accent from any country. Group guesses. Wrong guess = you take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever lied about where you've traveled? Yes = 3 sips of fraud.
{player1}, what's the longest flight you've ever taken? Under 4 hours? Take 3 sips, amateur.
{player1}, have you ever gotten sick from food abroad? Yes = take 2 sips. No = you played it safe, take 1.
{player1}, what's the most money you've spent on a single trip? Over $2,000? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever cried at an airport? Yes = 2 sips. No = you're strong, take 1.
{player1}, name a country that starts with the same letter as your name. Can't? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever been to a country where you didn't speak the language at all? Yes = take 1 sip of bravery. No = take 2.
{player1}, describe a country without saying its name. Nobody guesses in 15 seconds? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, what's the best meal you've had while traveling? Tell it or take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever slept in an airport? Yes = take 2 sips. No = take 1 sip of privilege.
{player1}, what's the most overrated tourist attraction you've visited? Take 2 sips for the hot take.
{player1}, have you ever been stopped by customs? Yes = take 3 sips and explain.
{player1}, what's the worst hotel you've ever stayed in? Tell it or take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever traveled solo? Yes = give out 2 sips of independence. No = take 2.
{player1}, show the group your passport photo. Refuse = 4 sips.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would be the worst travel buddy.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever would definitely lose their passport.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever has the most basic travel bucket list.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever would complain the most about jet lag.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would be the worst at navigating a foreign city.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever packs the most unnecessary stuff.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would be the pickiest eater abroad.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips to whoever would overpay for everything as a tourist.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever would panic the most if they lost their phone abroad.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever has never tried food from another culture.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever takes the most photos on vacation instead of enjoying it.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would get scammed first in a foreign market.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips to whoever has the weakest passport (fewest visa-free countries).
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever complains the most about walking.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would refuse to eat street food.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever only vacations at resorts.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would try to haggle and fail miserably.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips to whoever would be the loudest American abroad.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever would get lost first in Tokyo's subway system.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would accidentally offend someone in a foreign country.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever judges other people's travel photos the most.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would rather stay at the hotel than explore.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever books the cheapest flights with the worst layovers.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips to whoever claims they're 'well-traveled' but has only been to Cancún.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would be the most dramatic about a delayed flight.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever would spend the entire trip on their phone.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever refuses to try new things when traveling.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever would bring a suitcase for a weekend trip.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips to whoever sunburns the fastest on vacation.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would miss their flight because they were shopping at duty-free.
{player1}, give out as many sips as countries you've visited. Max 6. Zero? Take 3.
{player1}, give out as many sips as continents you've been to. Max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as languages you can say 'hello' in. Max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as passports you've had (including expired). Max 4.
{player1}, give out as many sips as flights you've taken this year. Max 6. Zero? Take 2.
{player1}, give out as many sips as times you've been to Mexico or Canada. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as countries you can name in Africa. Divide by 5, max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as stamps in your passport. Divide by 5, max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as road trips you've taken. Max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as times you've slept in a hostel. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as states you've visited. Divide by 5, max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as times you've used a translation app abroad. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as foreign cuisines you cook at home. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as national parks you've visited. Max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as islands you've been to. Max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as currencies you've used. Max 6.
{player1}, give out as many sips as travel shows/vlogs you watch regularly. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as times you've crossed a border by land. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as foreign languages you've attempted to learn. Max 5.
{player1}, give out as many sips as times you've been to an airport this year. Max 6.
Everyone who has a valid passport — give out 1 sip. Don't have one? Take 3.
Everyone who has been to Europe — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has been to Asia — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has been to South America — take 2 sips. Respect.
Everyone who has been to Africa — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has never left the country — take 3 sips. It's time.
Everyone who has slept in an airport — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has traveled to a country where they didn't speak the language — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has gotten food poisoning abroad — take {sip_count} sips of PTSD.
Everyone who has been on a cruise — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has tried an insect as food — take 2 sips. Give out 2 if you liked it.
Everyone who has been pickpocketed or scammed while traveling — take 3 sips.
Everyone who has taken a gap year or traveled for more than a month — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has been to more than 10 countries — give out 3 sips.
Everyone who has a travel bucket list — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has been on a road trip over 8 hours — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has stayed in a hostel — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has taken a train in Europe — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has been to a country that no longer exists under the same name — take 3 sips.
Everyone who has bartered/haggled in a foreign market — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has a friend from another country — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has cried at an airport — take {sip_count} sips of emotion.
Everyone who uses Google Translate when traveling — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has been to a UNESCO World Heritage site — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has hiked a mountain abroad — take 2 sips.
Everyone who prefers window seats — take 1 sip. Aisle? Also 1 sip. Middle? Take 3. You monster.
Everyone who has been to a country in the Southern Hemisphere — take 2 sips.
Everyone who can point to Bhutan on a map — give out 2 sips. Can't? Take 2.
Everyone who has tried durian — take 3 sips. You're brave.
Everyone who has visited a beach in another country — take 1 sip.
Everyone who has accidentally ordered something terrible in a foreign restaurant — take 2 sips.
Everyone who brings too many clothes when they travel — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has had a delayed flight of 4+ hours — take {sip_count} sips.
Everyone who has traveled with someone at this table — take 1 sip together.
Everyone who has said 'I could live here' while on vacation — take 2 sips. You couldn't.
Everyone who takes off their shoes on planes — take 3 sips. Everyone hates you.
Everyone who has been to a country most people haven't heard of — take 2 sips and name it.
Everyone who checks the weather at their destination daily before a trip — take 1 sip of anxiety.
Everyone who overpacks 'just in case' — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has visited more than 3 countries in one trip — give out 2 sips.
Everyone who has eaten McDonald's in a foreign country — take 2 sips of shame.
Everyone who posts 'take me back' on vacation photos — take {sip_count} sips.
Everyone who can name all 7 continents — give out 1 sip. Can't? Take 3.
Everyone who has a 'travel' Instagram highlight — take 2 sips.
Everyone who has ever used 'I've traveled' to win an argument — take 3 sips.
On 3, point to the best travel buddy here. They give out 3 sips.
On 3, point to the WORST travel buddy. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would get lost first in a foreign city. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to the most well-traveled person at the table. They give out 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would survive the best in the wilderness. They give out 2 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would overpay for everything while traveling. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would cause an international incident. They take 4 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would cry the most from homesickness. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would refuse to try local food. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would take the most selfies at every landmark. They take 2 sips.
On 3, point to the biggest geography nerd. They give out 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would be the most annoying on a long flight. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would pack the heaviest suitcase. They take 2 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would be most likely to miss a connecting flight. They take 4 sips.
On 3, point to whoever talks the most about their travels. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would be the best at haggling in a market. They give out 2 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would be the worst at reading a map. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would move abroad if they could. They take 2 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would book the cheapest, sketchiest accommodation. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever needs a vacation the most right now. They give out 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever has the most passport stamps. They give out 2 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would have the worst jet lag. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would try to speak the local language and butcher it. They take 2 sips.
On 3, point to whoever is the most culturally aware person here. They give out 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would be the most dramatic about a bug in their hotel room. They take 3 sips.
On 3, point to whoever would complain the most about airplane food. They take 2 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries back and forth. First to repeat or blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name capital cities. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries in Europe. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries in Africa. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name islands. First to hesitate drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name famous landmarks. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name world currencies. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name languages. First to hesitate takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries that start with 'S.' First to blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name cities in Asia. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name bodies of water (oceans, seas, rivers, lakes). First to blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name mountain ranges. First to hesitate takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name deserts. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name airlines. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries you've never been to. First to hesitate drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name UNESCO World Heritage Sites. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name national dishes from different countries. First to blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name flags with the color red. First to hesitate takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries in South America. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name cities that have hosted the Olympics. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries bordering the Mediterranean Sea. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name world religions. First to hesitate takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name types of cuisine (Thai, Italian, etc.). First to blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries with 4 letters or fewer. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name famous bridges. First to blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries in the Middle East. First to hesitate takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name famous travel destinations. First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Who has been to more countries? Fewer = {sip_count} sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name national parks (any country). First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name former names of countries (e.g., Persia = Iran). First to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries on the equator. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries by their flag colors — {player1} says a country, {player2} names a flag color. Switch. First wrong takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name seas. First to hesitate drinks 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries where English is an official language. First to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1} vs {player2}: Name countries that border China. First to blank drinks 3 sips.
{player1} is the Pilot. They choose a 'destination' each round and everyone must toast to it. Refuse = 2 sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s flight has landed. Pilot duties over.

prompts.text: Everyone must say 'cheers' in a different language before each sip for {rule_turns} rounds. Repeat a language = 2 sips.
No saying any country names for {rule_turns} rounds. Slip = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Tour Guide. They narrate what's happening like a guided tour for {rule_turns} rounds.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s tour has ended. Please exit through the gift shop.

prompts.text: Everyone must add 'mate' to the end of every sentence, Australian style, for {rule_turns} rounds. Forget = 1 sip.
Must speak in an accent for {rule_turns} rounds. {player1} picks the accent. Break character = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Accent rule expired. Normal voices return.

prompts.text: No English for {rule_turns} rounds. Use any other language, gibberish, or gestures. English = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Customs Officer. They can 'inspect' anyone's drink and make them take a sip once per round for {rule_turns} rounds.
Must refer to everyone by a country name instead of their real name for {rule_turns} rounds. Real name = 2 sips.
Before each sip, name a country you haven't named yet this game. Repeat = 2 sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
Must gesture/point in the direction of the country whenever one is mentioned for {rule_turns} rounds. Wrong direction = 1 sip.
{player1} is the Ambassador. They can 'veto' one sip assignment per round for {rule_turns} rounds.
Everyone must say a fun fact before drinking for {rule_turns} rounds. Boring fact = extra sip.
Half the sips! All sip counts are halved (round up) for {rule_turns} rounds. Duty-free discount.
Double passport stamp! All sip counts are doubled for {rule_turns} rounds.
prompts.rule_end_text: Double stamps over. Single sips return.

prompts.text: {player1} is the Flight Attendant. They decide the order people drink in for {rule_turns} rounds.
{player1}, name 5 countries in Africa in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 countries in Asia in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 countries in South America in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 European capitals in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 landlocked countries in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 island nations in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: countries that start with 'M.' Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 world currencies in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: famous landmarks. Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 countries that border France in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 languages in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: national dishes (name the country and dish). Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, point to where Japan is on an imaginary map. Group judges. Way off = 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 countries that start with 'Z' in 10 seconds or take 4 sips.
{player1}, start a category: rivers. Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 airlines in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: countries with blue in their flag. Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 countries in the Middle East in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: seas and oceans. Go around — first to fail drinks 2 sips.
{player1}, name the 7 continents in 10 seconds or take 4 sips.
{player1}, do a 15-second travel pitch for {player2}'s dream destination. Bad pitch = 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 deserts in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: countries with only 4 letters. Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 countries on the Mediterranean in 15 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 countries most people can't find on a map in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: mountain ranges. Go around — first to fail drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, say 'thank you' in 3 different languages in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 countries that drive on the left in 15 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 countries that have changed their name in 10 seconds or take 4 sips.
{player1}, start a category: places you should visit before you die. Go around — first to fail drinks 2 sips.
{player1}, your flight got cancelled. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, you lost your luggage. Take 5 sips.
{player1}, customs rejected you. Take 4 sips.
{player1}, you got food poisoning abroad. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, your passport expired. Take 5 sips.
{player1}, you missed your connection. Take 4 sips.
{player1}, you got pickpocketed. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, you're stuck in a middle seat for 12 hours. Take 5 sips.
{player1}, your hotel lost your reservation. Take 4 sips.
{player1}, you ate something you can't identify. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, your taxi took the long way. Take 4 sips.
{player1}, you got sunburned on day one. Take 5 sips.
{player1}, your travel partner bailed. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, you fell asleep and missed your stop. Take 4 sips.
{player1}, you forgot to bring a charger to a foreign country. Take 5 sips.
Heaven! Last person to point up drinks 3 sips. Turbulence!
Floor! Last person to point down drinks 3 sips. Emergency landing!
{player1}, Would You Rather: never travel again OR drink 5 sips right now?
Social! Everyone clinks and takes 2 sips. Cheers from around the world.
Go around the table — everyone says a country. Any repeat = 3 sips for the repeater.
{player1}, spin an imaginary globe. Wherever your finger 'lands,' describe that place in 10 seconds. Bad description = 4 sips.
Passport check! Everyone show your phone's last vacation photo. No photos? Take 3 sips.
{player1} picks a country. Everyone must say one fact about it. Can't? Take 2 sips.
Language round! Go around — everyone says 'cheers' in a different language. Repeat or blank = 3 sips.
Customs declaration! Everyone confesses one weird thing they've brought back from a trip. No trip? Take 3 sips.
Everyone guess where {player1} would move if they left the country. Wrong? Take 3 sips.
Everyone guess {player1}'s dream vacation destination. Wrong? Take 2 sips. {player1} takes 2 if nobody gets it.
Everyone guess what country {player1} would survive the longest in with no phone or money. Wrong? Take 2 sips.
Everyone guess what language {player1} would learn if they had to pick one tomorrow. Wrong? Take 2 sips.
Everyone guess the farthest place {player1} has ever traveled from home. Wrong? Take 3 sips.
Everyone guess what city {player1} would choose for a one-month solo trip. Wrong? Take 2 sips.
Everyone guess what country {player1} would refuse to visit. Wrong? Take 2 sips. {player1} takes 3 if they can't name one.
Everyone guess what {player1} would pack first for a last-minute trip. Wrong? Take 2 sips.
Everyone guess where {player1} went on their most recent trip. Wrong? Take 2 sips. Never traveled? {player1} takes 4.
Everyone guess what food {player1} would fly across the world just to eat again. Wrong? Take 2 sips.
{player1}, tell your most embarrassing travel story. No story? Take 4 sips.
{player1}, describe the sketchiest place you've ever slept while traveling. Can't think of one? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, tell the group about a time you were completely lost in a foreign place. Nothing? Take 4 sips.
{player1}, what's the weirdest food you've tried abroad? Describe it. Never tried anything weird? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, tell the group about your worst travel companion experience. No story? Take 4 sips.
{player1}, what's the biggest culture shock you've ever experienced? Can't answer? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, tell the group about a time a stranger helped you while traveling. Nothing comes to mind? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, what's the longest you've been stuck at an airport or border? No story? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, if you could take one person here on a trip anywhere — who and where? They take 3 sips.
{player1}, pick someone here you'd trust to plan your entire vacation. They take 2 sips. Everyone else — you're not trusted. Take 1.
{player1}, where would you go if you had a one-way ticket and no responsibilities? Pick someone to come with you — they take 3 sips.
{player1}, name a country you'd want to retire in. Pick someone here who'd visit you — they take 2 sips.
{player1}, if you won a free trip for two, who at this table are you taking? They take 3 sips. Everyone else — take 1 for not making the cut.
{player1}, what's one place everyone says is amazing that you have zero interest in visiting? Anyone here who's been there takes 2 sips.
{player1}, pick someone here you think has the best travel stories. They tell one now or take 4 sips.
{player1}, name the most overrated tourist destination. Anyone here who's been there takes 2 sips.
{player1}, beach vacation or mountain trip — pick one. Everyone who disagrees takes 2 sips.
{player1}, what's a country everyone loves that you think is overhyped? Anyone here who's been there takes 2 sips.
{player1}, window seat or aisle seat — pick one. Everyone who disagrees takes 2 sips.
{player1}, all-inclusive resort or backpacking — pick one. Everyone on the other side takes 2 sips.
